deck: Dr. Charles Elliott of Cure Violence Global on treating violence like an epidemic, and how a thoughtful data strategy built on CommCare helps frontline workers earn credit, build trust, and prove their impact.
seo_description: Cure Violence Global's Dr. Charles Elliott on treating violence as a public health issue and using data and CommCare to track interruption, behavior, and norm change.
og_description: How Cure Violence Global uses a public health approach and CommCare data to prevent violence and empower frontline workers.

pull_quote.text: I think one of the main things that people need is to adopt a system that helps people get the credit for what they do. It's like silencing an entire army of people who have really important dialogue.
pull_quote.cite: Dr. Charles Elliott on data as empowerment

bullets: Violence as an epidemic: how Cure Violence applies disease control and epidemic reversal strategies to prevent violence in communities worldwide
Credible messengers: why culturally sensitive outreach workers and violence interrupters are the heart of the model, with their credibility as their shield
Measuring the hard to measure: tracking interruption, behavior change, and norm change across hundreds of indicators in CommCare
Data that empowers: how a common system lets local programs get credit, leverage funding, and tell their own story
Privacy by design: collecting no names or addresses to protect participants and build community trust, with evidence that trust in police rose in places like Brooklyn
From the field to the dashboard: translating ground-level realities up to national stakeholders, the airplane at every altitude
A rapid international build: customizing the system for a national program in Uruguay on a two-week timeline

participants each outreach worker builds a relationship with
timeline to customize and implement the system for the Uruguay national program

guests.name: Dr. Charles Elliott
guests.role: Director of Data Management, leading the data strategy and CommCare-powered systems behind the organization's violence prevention work.
